ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF MEDICAL RECORDS

DISCOVERY 610, APPEARANCES: STACEY LUCAS, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY FOR DEFENDANT AND MOVING PARTY: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND TRENT RHORER BETH A. HUBER FOR PLAINTIFF AND OPPOSING PARTY: JANICE ANDERSON-SANTOS HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF SPECIFIED MEDICAL RECORDS IS TIMELY AS THE RECORDS PERTAIN TO THE PLAINTIFF. CCP SECTION 1985.3(G) DOES NOT IMPOSE A DEADLINE BY WHICH TO BRING A MOTION TO COMPEL COMPLIANCE WITH A SUBPOENA DUCES TECUM DIRECTED TO THE RECORDS OF A PARTY TO THE ACTION. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO ANY RECORDS PERTAINING TO THE CONDITIONS DESCRIBED IN PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSE TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORY SET #1, #51, FROM 1992 TO THE PRESENT. THE PARTIES ARE TO ENTER INTO A STIPULATED PROTECTIVE ORDER TO PROHIBIT THE DISSEMINATION OR USE OF SAID RECORDS FOR PURPOSES UNRELATED TO THE PRESENT LITIGATION. PRODUCTION OF THE REQUESTED RECORDS SHALL BE COMPLETED ON OR BEFORE FEBRUARY 14, 2005. FURTHER PROCEEDINGS AS TO SCOPE OF DISCLOSURE OF SPECIFIED RECORDS SET FOR FEB-16-05 AT 9:00 AM IN DEPT. 610. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

IN ADDITION, THE COURT FINDS THAT PLAINTIFF FAILED TO MAKE A REASONABLE AND GOOD FAITH EFFORT TO INFORMALLY RESOLVE THE MATTERS IN DISPUTE BEFORE FILING THE INSTANT MOTION. BRUCE E. CHAN.
